discovered_later_policy: Allowed only for files inside the UI Lab generated-runtime boundary, the shared UI variant contract, their focused tests, or the active authority documents, with a recorded rationale and no evidence-class widening. Manager, database, provider, Hermes, migration, and infrastructure paths are never discovered-later.
implementation_steps: Write tests/unit/ui-variant-runtime-contract.test.ts first: admission truth table over the three registry manifests times both surfaces times acknowledgement, capability projection grants and withholds, and every intent rejection code.

Add packages/shared/src/ui-variant-runtime.ts with admitUiVariantForRuntime, projectExperienceModelForVariant, and resolveUiVariantIntent; add UiVariantHostMethod and UiVariantIntentAudit types to ui-variant.ts and export the module from index.ts.
Route UiVariantHost through the admission decision: refusal panel for denied variants, forced reference-client slot when the manifest requires it, and a persistent candidate banner under lab review.

Route UiLabShell through projectExperienceModelForVariant and resolveUiVariantIntent, add the live variants navigation entry, and reduce LiveEmployeeProvider.dispatchVariantIntent to a thin executor over the resolved host method without weakening any Phase 1 fail-closed path.
Add the operator acknowledgement search parameter to the live variant route and a live variants chooser route that shows each manifest's admission decision and reason.
Extend scripts/verify-ui-architecture.mjs with generated-runtime singleton and wiring checks, and scripts/ui-variant.mjs with the additional forbidden browser-capability patterns.

Extend the Phase 1 workbench and architecture fitness tests, wire the new suite into test:ui:contracts, then reconcile ADR, Phase 2 doc, UI Lab README, CODEGRAPH, authority map, decision router, and memory with exact evidence.

maximum_patch.expansion_rule: If the 26-file envelope is exceeded, stop and re-admit with a recorded discovered_later rationale before evaluating. Do not widen evidence class.

predictions.claim: The generated runtime refuses live employee data to deprecated, experiment, and lab_only variants, admits approved variants directly, and admits candidate variants on the live surface only under explicit operator acknowledgement.
predictions.falsifier: A lab_only or experiment manifest is admitted on the live_owner_workbench surface, or a candidate manifest is admitted without acknowledgement.

predictions.claim: A variant receives only the EmployeeExperienceModelV1 capabilities its manifest declares in required or optional; undeclared and intentionally omitted capabilities are neutral empty values.
predictions.falsifier: A capability absent from the manifest declaration reaches the variant component with live employee content.

predictions.claim: Every dispatched variant intent resolves to a closed UiVariantHostMethod union or a typed rejection code, and no fixture intent or high-risk direct intent executes on the live surface.
predictions.falsifier: A dispatched intent reaches a live Manager route without a declared model intent and an allowed host method.

predictions.claim: The generated runtime is implementable from ResourcePayload and the existing owner projection alone, with no Manager mutation, connector custody, or non-ResourcePayload employee read.
predictions.falsifier: A variant or the runtime requires non-ResourcePayload employee data, connector custody, or authority/approval/effect mutation.

predictions.claim: Phase 1 live workbench contracts, the UI architecture verifier, the UI system validator, and web typecheck stay green on the exact candidate.
predictions.falsifier: npm run test:ui:contracts, node scripts/verify-ui-architecture.mjs, node scripts/validate-ui-system.mjs, or npm run typecheck --workspace @amtech/web fails.

selected_candidates.summary: Add one shared, pure generated-runtime contract (admission policy, capability-scoped model projection, typed intent bridge over a closed host-method union) and route the existing UI Lab variant host, shell, provider, and routes through it; extend the architecture and variant verifiers with generated-runtime isolation checks.

selected_candidates.rejection: Policy would live in browser components, could not be unit-tested independently of React, and would leave Phase 3/4 without a typed host-method attachment point.
selected_candidates.summary: Enforce admission and projection inline inside apps/web components without a shared contract module.

selected_candidates.rejection: The Phase 2 spec rejects a second employee model; two models would split identity and let a variant read outside ResourcePayload.
selected_candidates.summary: Introduce a second employee model dedicated to generated variants alongside EmployeeExperienceModelV1.

selected_candidates.rejection: Variants are first-party repository source compiled into the Web bundle, not negotiated remote ui:// resources; CCIP-6.8 isolation applies to MCP Apps, and an iframe transport would add a message boundary without removing the actual gap, which is unenforced manifest policy. Recorded as the escalation path if variants ever become non-first-party.
selected_candidates.summary: Render variants inside a sandboxed opaque-origin iframe with postMessage, mirroring the MCP Apps host.

selected_candidates.rejection: Documentation is not a boundary; a lab_only experiment variant would still receive live owner approvals and conversation at runtime.
selected_candidates.summary: Baseline: document the admission and capability policy in the variant standard and leave enforcement to review.

stop_conditions: Stop and replan if a variant or the runtime needs non-ResourcePayload employee data, connector custody, or authority/approval/effect mutation.
Stop if implementation would need a new Manager endpoint, browser credential custody, migration, provider adapter, or Hermes change.
Do not promote any variant manifest, UI Lab preset, or fixture to satisfy admission.
Do not weaken an existing Phase 1 contract assertion or verifier check to obtain green; strengthen or preserve the invariant instead.
Do not claim P4. Record live browser acceptance, provider acceptance, deployment, pilot, and production as blockers when unavailable.
Stop and re-admit if the patch exceeds 26 files.
